conbody.p: Read this section to learn about new features in Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 21.4.

ul.li: JCNR-Controller–The JCNR-controler is based on our tried-and-tested containerized routing protocol daemon, cRPD. We use cRPD in the JCNR to provide the control plane functions, interface with the forwarding plane, and manage routing protocol traffic to and from other networks, containers, and Kubernetes pods.

li.p: JCNR-vRouter–JCNR-vRouter is a DPDK-based forwarding plane. JCNR-vRouter is able to perform the following services: provide high-speed forwarding, provide network services such as installing and enforcing security policies, and perform routing with Layer 3 VPNs.
JCNR-vrouter uses a component known as the vRouter-agent to receive communication from JCNR-control-plane and JCNR-CNI.

ul.li: JCNR-CNI–JCNR uses the new container network interface application, JCNR-CNI.

li.p: Dual Stack Pod Network (IPv4/IPv6)–In JCNR, the pod network is divided into an IPv6 underlay network and an IPv4/IPv6 overlay network. The IPv6 underlay network is used for control plane traffic such as BGP, OSPF, and ISIS.
The IPv4/IPv6 overlay network is used for traffic sent and received by the user pods created in the compute nodes connected to the JCNR-vRouter.

Support for BGP, OSPF, and ISIS Routing Protocols–The JCNR-control-plane uses an IPv6 underlay network supported, in turn, by the JCNR-vRouter data plane. Control plane traffic such as BGP, OSPF, and ISIS protocols are routed over this IPv6 underlay network.
Support for SR-MPLS–JCNR supports SR-MPLS (over IPv4 and IPv6) tunnels, allocated by the JCNR-control-plane, that the JCNR-vRouter uses to route overlay traffic coming from user pods.

IPv4/IPv6 Security Groups–JCNR controls the types of traffic that can pass through any network port with the use of security groups and rules. Both ingress and egress traffic can be controlled using these security groups.
Netconf-SSH–JCNR-control-plane configuration is performed using Netconf-SSH (NETCONF protocol over SSH).
